Summary: Essential content of InteractiveDemo.java

Purpose  
Non-blocking CLI that lets a user chat with the Ganglia agent; the agent can pause mid-workflow to ask the user a question (ask_selection) and resume after the answer.

Key mechanics  
- Vert.x event-loop tuned to 60s max execute/block times to survive long reasoning.  
- Dedicated “stdin-reader-thread” publishes every typed line to EventBus address “user.input.stdin”; typing “exit/quit” shuts the JVM.  
- Ganglia.bootstrap(vertx) succeeds → create TerminalUI, generate sessionId, start single UI stream listener.  
- Main loop: waitForInput() → ganglia.agentLoop().run(userInput, context) → handleAgentResponse().  
- handleAgentResponse() checks SessionContext for unanswered tool calls (hasPendingInteraction).  
  – If any exist: print “[INTERACTION REQUIRED]”, wait for next stdin line, then agentLoop().resume(null, feedback, context).  
  – Otherwise: print “--- Workflow Complete ---”, prompt for next turn.

No errors or warnings in the file; code is ready-to-run demo of interactive, resumable agent workflows.
Use recall_memory tool to view full content.
